restoreMissingTabs.description: Defines whether to restore tabs that are missed in the original location.

applicationCertificate.description: Specifies the SSL certificate configured for the Azure AD application that you want to use for data restore. You must provide the certificate as a Base64 string.

userName.description: Specifies the user name that you want to use for authenticating to the organization.

tabs.description:

**Note**: If you omit this property, all backed-up tabs of the specified channel will be restored.

restoreChangedTabs.description: Defines whether to restore tabs that have been modified in the original location since the time when the backup was created.

restoreMissingTabs.description: Defines whether to restore tabs that are missed in the original location.

reason.description: Specifies a reason for the restore operation.

displayName.description: Display name of the backed-up team.

groupEmail.description: Email address of the backed-up team.

isArchived.description: Defines whether the team is archived.

teams.description: Specifies IDs of teams that you want to restore. For more information on how to get such IDs, see [Get Teams](#tag/TeamsTeam/operation/TeamsTeam_Get).

restoreChangedItems.description: Defines whether to restore team items that have been modified in the original location since the time when the backup was created.

restoreMissingItems.description: Defines whether to restore team items that are missed in the original location.

restoreMembers.description: Defines whether to restore members of the restored team along with their roles.

restoreSettings.description: Defines whether to restore settings of the restored team.

impersonationAccountName.description:
Specifies a user name of the account that will be used as a team owner account to restore a team. This may be useful if you restore a previously removed team at the time when the original team owner account does not exist in Microsoft 365.

**Note**: This property is required if you want to use an application certificate for data restore. Use this property only with the `applicationCertificate` property. If you omit this property, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 will restore a team using an original team owner account from the backup.

userCode.description:
Specifies the authentication code. For more information on how to get a device code, see [Get Device Code](#tag/RestoreSession/operation/RestoreSession_DeviceCodeAction).
This property is required if you want to use a device code for data restore.

applicationId.description: Specifies the ID of the Azure AD application that you want to use for restore.

applicationCertificate.description: Specifies the SSL certificate configured for the Azure AD application that you want to use for data restore. You must provide the certificate as a Base64 string.

userName.description: Specifies the user name that you want to use for authenticating to the organization.

teams.description: Specifies IDs of teams that you want to restore. For more information on how to get such IDs, see [Get Teams](#tag/TeamsTeam/operation/TeamsTeam_Get).

restoreChangedItems.description: Defines whether to restore team items that have been modified in the original location since the time when the backup was created.

restoreMissingItems.description: Defines whether to restore team items that are missed in the original location.

restoreMembers.description: Defines whether to restore members of the restored team along with their roles.

restoreSettings.description: Defines whether to restore settings of the restored team.

reason.description: Specifies a reason for the restore operation.

impersonationAccountName.description:
Specifies a user name of the account that will be used as a team owner account to restore a team. This may be useful if you restore a previously removed team at the time when the original team owner account does not exist in Microsoft 365.

**Note**: This property is required if you want to use an application certificate for data restore. Use this property only with the `applicationCertificate` property. If you omit this property, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 will restore a team using an original team owner account from the backup.

preferredDataLocation.description: Preferred data location to create a new M365 group for a multi-geo tenant.

allowExternalSenders.description: Defines whether people external to the organization can send messages to the group.

autoSubscribeNewMembers.description: Defines whether new members added to the group will be auto-subscribed to receive email notifications.

allowCustomMemes.description: Defines whether users are allowed to include custom memes.

allowGiphy.description: Defines whether usage of Giphy is allowed.

allowStickersAndMemes.description: Defines whether users are allowed to include stickers and memes.

allowCreateUpdateChannels.description: Defines whether guests are allowed to add and update team channels.

allowDeleteChannels.description: Defines whether guests are allowed to delete team channels.

allowCreatePrivateChannel.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to add and update private channels.

allowCreateUpdateChannel.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to add and update channels.

allowDeleteChannel.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to delete channels.

allowAddRemoveApp.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to add and remove apps.

allowCreateUpdateRemoveTab.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to add, update, and remove tabs.

allowCreateUpdateRemoveConnector.description: Defines whether team members are allowed to add, update, and remove connectors.

allowUserEditMessage.description: Defines whether users are allowed to edit their messages.

allowUserDeleteMessage.description: Defines whether users are allowed to delete their messages.

allowOwnerDeleteMessage.description: Defines whether team owners are allowed to delete any message.

enableNotification.description: Defines whether Veeam Explorer will send email notifications.

server.description: Specifies the full DNS name or IP address of the SMTP server for sending email notifications.

port.description: Specifies the port used for connection to the SMTP server.

from.description: Specifies email address of the notification sender.

useAuthentication.description: Defines whether the SMTP server requires authentication.

useSSL.description: Defines whether Veeam Explorer uses a secure connection to transmit email notifications.

username.description: Specifies the user name of the account used for authentication to the SMTP server.

query.description: Specifies query parameters used to search for items. For the complete list of supported query parameters, see the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 REST API Reference Overview, section [Appendix A. Item Search Parameters](https://helpcenter.veeam.com/docs/vbo365/rest/appendix_search.html?ver=70).

offset.description: Excludes from a response page the first N items of a collection resource. The default value is *0*.
